name: CheckSmart
description_short: CheckSmart Taylor, Michigan — Ohio-based financial services chain offering check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, bill pay, and in-store access to...
description_long:
CheckSmart is a brick-and-mortar financial services chain operating multiple locations across Ohio, including several in the Columbus metro area. The company has positioned itself as a neighborhood financial center for consumers who need in-person access to basic financial services and short-term lending solutions. Their Columbus store on Morse Road sits on the northeast corner of Morse Rd and Cleveland Ave, with extended hours six days a week.

CheckSmart's core in-store services include check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill pay. They also offer Green Dot® Visa® Debit Cards and will exchange unused gift cards for cash. On the lending side, CheckSmart does not originate loans directly — instead, they provide Ohio residents with access to loan products from an unaffiliated third-party lender, acting as an introduction point rather than the creditor. Applicants are required to bring a government-issued ID, proof of income or employment, a working phone number, a valid email address, and proof of address.

What distinguishes CheckSmart is its role as a one-stop financial services storefront for underbanked consumers. Unlike purely online lenders, CheckSmart offers face-to-face service with staff who can guide applicants through the process. Online appointment scheduling is available to reduce wait times. Friday hours extend to 8:00 PM — later than most competing check-cashing locations — and Sunday service is available from 11:00 AM to 5:00 PM, which is unusual for this category.

Honestly, CheckSmart's loan offering is opaque by design: the company explicitly states it connects customers to an 'unaffiliated lender,' meaning borrowers should expect to deal with a separate entity whose terms, APRs, and policies are not disclosed on the CheckSmart website. Consumers shopping for loans should treat CheckSmart as a referral channel, not a direct lender, and should ask about the actual lender's rates and terms before signing anything. For check cashing and related services, CheckSmart fills a legitimate need but fee schedules are not published online.
pros: Multiple Ohio locations including five in Columbus metro area alone
Online appointment scheduling available to speed up in-store loan process
Extended Friday hours (8:00 AM – 8:00 PM) and Sunday availability (11:00 AM – 5:00 PM)
Green Dot® Visa® Debit Card available for purchase in-store
Gift card exchange for cash — converts unused gift cards to spendable money
Full-service financial hub: check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill pay under one roof
In-person staff assistance throughout the loan application process
cons: CheckSmart is not the actual lender — loans come from an undisclosed unaffiliated third party, so terms and APRs are unknown before you visit
Ohio-only physical presence; no service available outside the state
No loan rates, fees, or APR ranges published on the website — zero cost transparency
In-person visit required; no fully online application or funding path
Limited Saturday hours (9:00 AM – 4:00 PM) may be inconvenient for working borrowers
best_for: Ohio residents who need check cashing, money orders, or wire transfers in person
Underbanked consumers who prefer face-to-face guidance when applying for a short-term loan
People who need to exchange unused gift cards for cash
Consumers who want a single storefront for multiple basic financial services
diagnosis: CheckSmart is best suited for Ohio consumers who want in-person financial services — especially check cashing, money orders, and wire transfers — and may also need a referral to a short-term lender. The key caveat: CheckSmart does not lend its own money; it connects customers to an unaffiliated lender whose rates and terms are not disclosed upfront, so borrowers must ask about the actual loan cost before committing.
services: Green Dot® Visa® Debit Card (in-store purchase)
Short-term loan access via third-party lender
Online appointment scheduling for in-store visits
